plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-210 RP06.P02.M08.S19-RP06.P03.M05.S22 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-210 implements descriptor-only coverage that closes RP06.P02 (M08 unit-test tail, M09 Claude Review Packet cycle, M10 Closeout And Next Handoff cycle) and opens the RP06.P03 API-surface foundation (Scope Inventory, Contract Draft, Type And Shape Definition, Primary Implementation Slice, Secondary Workflow Slice, Permission And Audit Binding) as nine descriptor sections over 150 planned rows, with Hermes evidence, Claude review packet, and CP00-211 handoff, without opening API handler execution, API response serving, DMS runtime dispatch, golden case or Hermes packet runtime, rollback/retry runtime, unit-test runtime paths, review/approval route runtime, state writes, idempotency persistence, lock acquisition, workflow persistence, object storage, OCR, search, email, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-210 is a clean, internally consistent descriptor-only pack (RP06 DMS core, P02 closeout + P03 API-surface foundation, 150 units, range RP06.P02.M08.S19-RP06.P03.M05.S22). The pack does NOT over-claim: manifest/command-evidence set production_ready=false at status implemented_pending_review, with production_ready_candidate=true reflecting only that coverage+foundation validations pass, gated behind this review and human final approval. All layers agree (prompt baseline, review-baseline, registry bindings, dms-core-contract.json, README, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, live closeout-pack-plan.json). Distribution math is exact across phase (46+104=150), deliverable (8 types=150), micro-title (9=150), and per-section row lists (150); each dimension is independently validated in validators.js and asserted in model.test.js, with npm test / rp06:dms-core:validate / validate recorded at exit 0. Descriptor-only, no-write, and no-real-data boundaries are uniformly enforced per row and at pack level (no API handler execution, no Hermes runtime receipts, no response/payload/cursor/unauthorized-data leakage). Authority boundary is upheld: Claude is read-only and not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false), local validation does not claim enterprise trust, and runtime opening requires human approval in CP00-211+. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor/receipt handling, not product runtime. Review-hardening blockers (auth_failure, stdout_0_bytes, malformed_json, fenced_json_plus_prose, tool_call_shaped_output, plus final-approval/source-mutation/enterprise-trust claims) are encoded and mirrored in the contract; readiness auth-preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON and tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob. Two non-blocking P3 observations recorded for downstream/human-approval awareness; no P0/P1/P2 issues. Pack is supported for closeout under the standing human-final-approval boundary.

findings.title: P03 API-surface sections are descriptor stubs; asserted no-leak guards are deferred design intent to enforce at runtime opening
findings.details: The six RP06.P03 sections (M00 Scope Inventory, M01 Contract Draft, M02 Type And Shape Definition, M03 Primary Implementation Slice, M04 Secondary Workflow Slice, M05 Permission And Audit Binding) declare API-surface rows (request/response contracts, error-code taxonomy, pagination/filtering contract, serialization guard, unauthorized-data omission, API fixtures) as descriptor-only stubs. Guards such as api_response_payload_included=false, pagination_cursor_material_included=false, unauthorized_data_omitted=true, and serialization_guard_applied=true are asserted as static descriptor flags, not exercised against any executing handler. This is correctly disclosed (downstream_consumer_note.downstream_runtime_opened=false; authority_boundary.human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-211), so it is not a defect for this descriptor pack.
findings.recommendation: When CP00-211+ opens runtime for these P03 API surfaces, the human final approver should verify the real handlers actually honor every no-leak guard asserted here (no API response payloads/cursor material/error-taxonomy internals/unauthorized data leaked; serialization guard applied). No action required for CP00-210 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Deferred to CP00-211+ runtime opening under human final approval; informational, no CP00-210 action.

findings.title: RP06.P02.M08 micro-title is 'Hermes Evidence Packet' while the 4 rows in this pack are all test-type tail rows
findings.details: Because the pack boundary starts mid-micro-phase at RP06.P02.M08.S19, the M08 section contributes only its 4 tail rows (Unit test: happy/denied/review, Integration smoke case), all deliverable_type=test, even though the M08 micro_title is 'Hermes Evidence Packet' (its evidence head rows S01-S18 closed in CP00-209). Separately, the 4 hermes_evidence deliverable-type units live in P03.M02-M05 (S14 'Hermes API evidence'). The micro_title and deliverable_type dimensions are orthogonal and both independently validated to count 4, so this is internally consistent and not a defect; it is noted only because scanning by micro_title alone could be momentarily confusing.
findings.recommendation: No change required. Optionally note in handoff docs that M08 in CP00-210 is the test tail of a micro-phase whose evidence rows were closed upstream, to aid future readers.
findings.post_review_disposition: Acknowledged as a correct mid-micro-phase boundary artifact; informational, no action required.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-210 dms core p02 closeout p03 foundation descriptor pack
